mnemonics.title: Friend Function Declaration
mnemonics.content: FRIN - Friend keyword, Return type, Interface (operator), Name of function

mnemonics.title: Operator Overloading Rules
mnemonics.content: SOFAR - Symmetry preserved, Overload only existing, Friends allowed, Arity unchanged, Restrictions apply (?:, ::, ., .*, sizeof)

mnemonics.title: Return Type for Chaining
mnemonics.content: REF - Return by Reference, Everything works, Flow continues

acronyms.expansion: Object Oriented Programming - uses classes and objects
acronyms.usage: Operator overloading is a feature of OOP in C++

acronyms.expansion: Input Output Stream - base class for cin and cout
acronyms.usage: ostream and istream are derived from ios for stream operations

visualTips.visualization: Draw a box representing class, with 'friend' keyword inside, and a dotted line connecting to an external function outside the box representing access to private members
Think of '+' operators as linking rings: a + b returns a reference that becomes the left operand for next + c
Draw arrow pointing from object to stream for <<, and from stream to object for >>

analogies.analogy: Like a person with a special key (friend function) that can enter a private house (class) even though they're not family (not a member)
Like addition: 3 + 5 equals 5 + 3. Friend functions ensure both orders work for user-defined types
Like passing a microphone to someone else - the microphone (reference) keeps being passed along for more input
